title: pleurostylia putamen
intro: Pleurostylia putamen, also known as the Indian plum or wild peach, is a tropical evergreen shrub native to the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Pleurostylia putamen include treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and improving immune function, as well as being used as a natural remedy for fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. Pleurostylia putamen can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
